top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Best Buddies Challenge: Miami's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
Best Buddies Challenge: Miami's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Best Buddies Challenge: Miami's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Best Buddies Challenge: Miami's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Best Buddies Challenge: Miami. Train it until it feels boring.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 100 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Best Buddies Challenge: Miami's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 100 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
At Best Buddies Challenge: Miami, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Best Buddies Challenge: Miami’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 100 miles of mixed terrain in Miami, Florida, USA.
